name: mdk4
summary: MDK is a proof-of-concept tool to exploit common IEEE 802.11 protocol weaknesses.

documentation.readme: MDK is a proof-of-concept tool to exploit common IEEE 802.11 protocol weaknesses.
usage_sections: Installation: apt-get install pkg-config libnl-3-dev libnl-genl-3-dev libpcap-dev git clone https://github.com/aircrack-ng/mdk4 cd mdk4 make sudo make install # Using Arch Linux (and derived) append CC=clang after any make in commands.

Features: - Supports two WiFi card (one for receiving data, another for injecting data). - Supports block the specified ESSID/BSSID/Station MAC in command option. - Supports both 2.4 to 5GHz (Linux). - supports IDS Evasion (Ghosting, Fragmenting, Does not fully work with every driver). - s

arguments.name: -A
arguments.description: set an AP MAC

arguments.name: -a
arguments.description: Use also non-printable caracters in generated SSIDs

arguments.name: -B
arguments.description: Specify an AP BSSID to attack.

arguments.name: -b
arguments.description: Select if 11 Mbit (b) or 54 MBit (g) networks are created

arguments.name: -c
arguments.description: Create fake networks on channel <chan>. If you want your card to

arguments.name: -E
arguments.description: Specify an AP ESSID to attack.

arguments.name: -e
arguments.description: SSID to probe for

arguments.name: -f
arguments.description: Read SSIDs from file

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: Hop to channel where network is spoofed

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: Add user-defined IE(s) in hexadecimal at the end of the tagged parameters

arguments.name: -l
arguments.description: Use Logoff messages to kick clients

arguments.name: -m
arguments.description: Use valid accesspoint MAC from built-in OUI database

arguments.name: -n
arguments.description: Use SSID <ssid> instead of randomly generated ones

arguments.name: -p
arguments.description: Continue bruteforcing, starting at <word>.

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: Probe request tests (mod-musket)

arguments.name: -S
arguments.description: Specify a station MAC address to attack.

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: Set speed in packets per second (Default: 50)

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: -t 1 = Create only Ad-Hoc network

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: Read MACs and SSIDs from file. See example file!

arguments.name: -W
arguments.description: Specify a whitelist station MAC.

arguments.name: -w
arguments.description: Select which type of encryption the fake networks shall have

arguments.name: -x
arguments.description: Enable full IDS stealth by matching all Sequence Numbers

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: apt-get install pkg-config libnl-3-dev libnl-genl-3-dev libpcap-dev; git clone https://github.com/aircrack-ng/mdk4. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `mdk4 <interface> <attack_mode> [attack_options]`. Further invocations from upstream: `mdk4 <interface in> <interface out> <attack_mode> [attack_options]`. MDK is a proof-of-concept tool to exploit common IEEE 802.11 protocol weaknesses.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: MDK is a proof-of-concept tool to exploit common IEEE 802.11 protocol weaknesses.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: MDK is a proof-of-concept tool to exploit common IEEE 802.11 protocol weaknesses.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Wireless, use mdk4 because MDK is a proof-of-concept tool to exploit common IEEE 802.11 protocol weaknesses.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `mdk4 <interface> <attack_mode> [attack_options]` as documented upstream for mdk4.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `mdk4 <interface in> <interface out> <attack_mode> [attack_options]` as documented upstream for mdk4.
When chaining Wireless tools, pair mdk4 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `mdk4 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
